blocks.body: Sentence structure is the basis of the English language. An English simple sentence is built according to a fixed scheme S–V–O (Subject–Verb–Object), unlike Ukrainian, where the order of words is more free.

{ "title": "10. Brief summary", "items": [ "A simple sentence has one subject and one predicate.", "Basic structure: S + V + O (Subject + Verb + Object).", "Subject — who or what performs the action; stands in the first place.", "Verb (predicate) - action or state; stands after the subject.", "Object (application) - what the action is aimed at; stands after the verb.", "In English, the order of words is fixed - elements cannot be interchanged.", "Some sentences have the structure S + V (without an adjunct): She sleeps." ] }
